expectations.text: The draft identifies the request as a bug ticket and uses the bug template headings: Description, Steps to Reproduce, Actual Behavior, Expected Behavior, and Additional Information.
expectations.evidence: Used the bug template (assets/bug.md) and produced a draft with Description, Steps to Reproduce, Actual Behavior, Expected Behavior, and Additional Information sections.

expectations.text: The response references the notifications implementation with concrete repo context such as NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsOrchestrator, NotificationRepository, or DefaultNotificationRepository.
expectations.evidence: Found concrete files: NotificationsViewModel.kt:80-83, NotificationsOrchestrator.kt:28-34, DefaultNotificationRepository.kt:174-177, NotificationRepository.kt:15, etc.

expectations.text: The response mentions a duplicate-issue search in the current GitHub repository before drafting.
expectations.evidence: Performed preflight (gh check) and duplicate check before drafting.

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: The agent stopped at draft/approval mode and did not create the issue; no body file was written to disk.

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: The title was not duplicated inside the body of the draft.

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: No body file was written (agent stopped at draft/approval mode without creating the issue), so the condition is trivially satisfied.

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations passed. The old_skill baseline correctly used the bug template, investigated concrete repo files, checked for duplicates, stopped at draft/approval mode, avoided duplicating the title in the body, and did not write a code-fenced body file.
